title: Comparative Genocide Lab — comparative analysis of mass political violence across ten canonical dimensions

fields.vision: A comparative genocide analysis chamber built around ten canonical dimensions of mass political violence.
fields.mechanism: It uses six zones: Charter Wall, Zone II Dimensional Spine, Zone III Case Forge, Method Chamber with coding rules, Causal and Typological Observatory, and Campaign Table. The lab distinguishes concepts from operational rules, preserves the ten canonical analytic dimensions without contradiction, standardizes case cards, preserves contested-case protocols, supports first case-forge work, cross-case observatory analysis, and sprint and gate status, and is designed to match MuranoOS-style room logic through React/TypeScript and Rust/Tauri implementation paths.
fields.built_components: v1.0 of the page is complete in the discipline-honest sense. The conceptual specification, the six-zone architecture, the ten-dimension spine with scoring anchors, the React/TypeScript scaffold (page shell plus seven components: CampaignTable, CaseDetailPanel, CaseLibrary, DimensionLibrary, MethodChamber, Observatory, RoomDashboard), the Rust/Tauri command module, the SQLite migration, and the three-layer test stack are all written and bundled. The handoff is honest about what it is not: a finished production implementation, with guaranteed-matching MuranoOS runtime symbols, in a deployed state. Implementation names (page-registration stub name, command names, runtime symbol references) are explicitly tagged as design assumptions and require verification against the live MuranoOS desktop runtime before integration. No CONTRADICTED items remain open in the v1.0 package.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: The forward path is integration into the live MuranoOS desktop and population of the Case Forge with real comparative cases under the Method Chamber discipline. The page is ready for the work; what it needs is real scholarship running through it rather than additional design. Step 01 Runtime-symbol verification Verify the assumed page-registration stub names, command names, and runtime symbol references against the live MuranoOS desktop application before wiring. Step 02 Live integration Wire the Rust commands, the SQLite migration, and the React scaffold into the running MuranoOS shell so the page renders inside MuranoOS. Step 03 Dimensional-spine population Populate each of the ten dimensions with conceptual definition, operational definition, subdimensions, indicators, and scoring anchors at the same level of fidelity. Step 04 First case-forge tranche First tranche of standardized case cards built against the spine, with all ten dimensions scored and confidence ratings recorded. Step 05 Observatory cross-case work Comparative work begins in the Observatory once enough cases populate the Forge — interaction matrix, typology development, indicator-warning matrix, prevention levers.
fields.status: Status: v1.0 of the page is complete in the discipline-honest sense. The conceptual specification, the six-zone architecture, the ten-dimension spine with scoring anchors, the React/TypeScript scaffold (page shell plus seven components: CampaignTable, CaseDetailPanel, CaseLibrary, DimensionLibrary, MethodChamber, Observatory, RoomDashboard), the Rust/Tauri command module, the SQLite migration, and the three-layer test stack are all written and bundled.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: the forward path is integration into the live MuranoOS desktop and population of the Case Forge with real comparative cases under the Method Chamber discipline. The page is ready for the work; what it needs is real scholarship running through it rather than additional design.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A research-page specification for comparative-genocide analysis built around the rule that nothing exists in isolation; every object must link to dimensions, cases, methods, evidence, process, or output work products. The page preserves losslessly the ten canonical analytic dimensions, the distinction between concept and operational rule, the distinction between settled and unsettled claims, the provenance of every major judgment, and the comparability of cases across the corpus. The provenance discipline is the strongest in the larger research bundle: every claim is tagged CONFIRMED , INFERRED , ASSUMED , or CONTRADICTED , and the page is engineered so that a team that did not witness the originating conversation can still work safely against its content.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: Six zones organize the page. The Charter Wall fixes scope and inclusion rules; the Dimensional Spine carries the ten canonical dimensions as living analytic objects; the Case Forge is where standardized case cards live; the Method Chamber holds the coding manual and contested-case protocol; the Causal and Typological Observatory is where comparative analysis happens; the Campaign Table tracks sprint and gate status.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A comparative genocide analysis chamber built around ten canonical dimensions of mass political violence.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It uses six zones: Charter Wall, Zone II Dimensional Spine, Zone III Case Forge, Method Chamber with coding rules, Causal and Typological Observatory, and Campaign Table. The lab distinguishes concepts from operational rules, preserves the ten canonical analytic dimensions without contradiction, standardizes case cards, preserves contested-case protocols, supports first case-forge work, cross-case observatory analysis, and sprint and gate status, and is designed to match MuranoOS-style room logic through React/TypeScript and Rust/Tauri implementation paths.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because comparative genocide analysis needs dimensions, cases, methods, evidence, process, and outputs linked together; otherwise cases become isolated moral descriptions rather than comparable analytical objects.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.
